title: festuca ovina var. novae-zelandiae
intro: Festuca ovina var. novae-zelandiae, commonly known as New Zealand blue grass or New Zealand sheep's fescue, is a variety of grass native to New Zealand, characterized by its blue-green foliage and slender stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Festuca ovina var. novae-zelandiae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
